refactor: decompose Studio UI monoliths (#114) (#141)

* refactor: decompose studio_handlers.py into _studio/ subpackage (Phase 1)

Extract 53 helper functions from the 2,717 LOC studio_handlers.py into 11
focused modules under routes/_studio/:

- ui_utils.py: toast helpers, type coercion
- manifest_helpers.py: manifest loading, source resolution, canvas helpers
- context.py: studio context normalization, recent hub, tab management
- thumbnail_cache.py: thumbnail sizing, pruning, remote cache management
- page_job_prefs.py: highres/stitch job preference persistence

- export_thumbnails.py: export thumbnail building and slice assembly

studio_handlers.py is now 1,045 LOC containing only route handlers.

Part of #114

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: decompose panes.py into panes/ subpackage (9 modules)

Split the 2,348-LOC monolithic panes.py into a panes/ subpackage with
one module per settings domain: pdf, viewer, system, network, general,
processing, images, thumbnails, discovery.

The __init__.py re-exports all _build_*_pane functions, so main.py
imports remain unchanged.

Each submodule carries only the imports it actually needs.

- pdf_inventory.py: PDF inventory panel

refactor: code quality improvements — dead code, DRY, and logic simplification (#55)

* refactor: remove dead code and unused placeholder modules

Delete coordinator/data_processing.py, error_handling.py, and listeners.py
— placeholder functions from a generic HA template, never imported.

Delete entity_utils/state_helpers.py — template code inspecting userId/title/body
fields irrelevant to venetian blinds; no callers outside the module.

Delete config_flow_handler/validators/sanitizers.py — sanitize_name() never
called; callers use .strip() inline.

Delete utils/validators.py — validate_api_response(), validate_config_value(),
and is_valid_url() never called.

Remove update_device_info() and get_device_identifiers() from
entity_utils/device_info.py — never called.

Clean up __init__.py re-exports in all affected packages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: extract _create_controller helper to fix apply_now missing args

apply_now.py created CoverController without first_facade_hit_this_cycle,
position_timeout_sec, or obstacle_was_blocking — the PR #50 obstacle
persistence fix never applied to the apply_now service action.

Extract _create_controller(hass, entry) to __init__.py and use it in
both apply_cover_tilts() and apply_now.py to ensure consistent args.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: centralise DeviceInfo construction via create_window_group_device_info

The same 6-line DeviceInfo(...) block was duplicated 4 times across
sensor/slat_sensors.py (x3) and switch/auto_control.py (x1).

Add create_window_group_device_info(config_entry) to
entity_utils/device_info.py and replace all 4 duplicates with one call.
Drop now-unused DeviceEntryType/DeviceInfo/DOMAIN imports from callers.

- Add _should_respect_manual_open() — encapsulates the manual-open
  bypass logic (first_facade_hit + obstacle_just_cleared exceptions)
- Add _apply_angle_constraints() — encapsulates per-cover min/max
  angle clamping before tilt inversion
- apply_calculation() is now significantly simpler to read

* refactor: replace no-sun if-chain with dispatch dict (Phase 6)

- Extract _no_sun_keep_last, _no_sun_open, _no_sun_close, _no_sun_set_to_percent

- _handle_no_sun dispatches via a dict[str, Callable] — each case is

* fix: regenerate diagrams post-merge instead of on PR branch

Committing SVGs directly to a PR branch via GITHUB_TOKEN doesn't
re-trigger pull_request CI (GitHub intentionally blocks this to
prevent recursion). This left CI permanently 'pending' after every
diagram update.

New approach: trigger on push to main, generate SVGs, open a small
chore PR that auto-merges once CI passes. Feature PRs are never
touched by this workflow.

fix(ss-inbound): loop on UDP decrypt error to re-register waker (#1309)

* fix(ss-inbound): loop on UDP decrypt error to re-register waker

When poll_recv_from_with_ctrl returns Poll::Ready(Err(e)) — e.g. an
SS2022 authentication failure from scan traffic or a brief password
mismatch during config refresh — the waker registered by the previous
poll_recv_from call was already consumed.  Returning Poll::Pending
without a new waker permanently suspends the UDP dispatch task (t1):
no further packets are ever processed, recv_q fills up, and every
subsequent UDP probe times out.

Fix: wrap the recv/match in a loop.  On error we fall through to the
next iteration; poll_recv_from_with_ctrl either reads the next queued
packet immediately or, if the socket is empty, returns Poll::Pending
task until new data arrives.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(dispatcher): preserve original dst IP before reverse_lookup for UDP

When reverse_lookup converts a packet's destination IP back to a domain
name (via the DNS cache), family_hint_for_session loses the original
address family and falls back to resolve_v6, which can return an IPv6
address for an originally-IPv4 destination.  This causes new_udp_socket
to create an IPv6 socket while bind_addr is still 0.0.0.0 (derived from
the IPv4 inbound source), resulting in EINVAL on bind.

Concrete case: TCP probe resolves cp.cloudflare.com -> 1.1.1.1 and
caches it.  UDP probe relays to 1.1.1.1:53; reverse_lookup finds
'cp.cloudflare.com' in cache -> domain; family_hint_for_session calls
resolve_v6('cp.cloudflare.com') -> 2606:4700:4700::1111 -> IPv6 socket
-> bind(0.0.0.0:0) -> EINVAL every minute.

Fix: stash the original destination IP in sess.resolved_ip before
reverse_lookup runs.  family_hint_for_session already checks
resolved_ip first and returns it directly, so it never reaches the
resolve_v6 fallback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ss inbound: replace peek_user_identity with post-handshake user_key()

Use shadowsocks-rust fork (Watfaq/shadowsocks-rust@dee2d932) which
exposes CryptoStream::user_key() and ProxyServerStream::user_key().

After the SS2022 handshake, look up the authenticated user by matching
socket.user_key() against the user manager — no manual BLAKE3 KDF +
AES-256-ECB peek needed. Removes the aes and blake3 dependencies from
the inbound TCP path and works for any cipher, not just
2022-blake3-aes-256-gcm.

The publish workflow was failing with NODE_AUTH_TOKEN empty because
the token-based publish is deprecated in favor of NPM OIDC trusted
publishers. Fix:

- Keep id-token: write permission and --provenance flag

NPM trusted publisher is already configured on npmjs.com for this
repo. OIDC federates identity from GitHub Actions → NPM, eliminating
the need for long-lived tokens.

* fix: payload passthrough for wing/room/original_text/valid_until in retrieval pipeline

Root cause: hybrid_search._vector_search() and memory_retrieval.retrieve_context()
were both hardcoded to cherry-pick only content/type/project/tags/timestamp from
Qdrant payload results. All spatial, temporal, and zero-loss fields were silently
dropped. Also fixed retrieve command threshold default (0.7 -> 0.45) and removed
stale imports causing UnboundLocalError in contradiction resolver.

* fix: complete contradiction resolver pipeline

Two root causes prevented contradiction resolution from working:
1. Empty query string in retrieve_context() produced zero-score vectors
   that fell below threshold, so no existing facts were ever found.
   Fix: use the new content as the search query.
2. Deprecated facts (valid_until set) were never filtered during search.
   Fix: add must_not range filter in hybrid_search._vector_search() to
   auto-exclude points where valid_until <= current time.

* fix: BM25 results now respect Qdrant payload filters (project/wing/room)

BM25 (SQLite FTS5) has no concept of Qdrant payload filters, so unscoped
results leaked into hybrid search when: 1) BM25-only results merged into
vector results without validation, and 2) the BM25-only fallback path
(when vector returns 0) returned results without any filter check.

Fix: Both paths now validate each BM25-only result against Qdrant via
a point lookup before including it. Also fixed the merge path to pass
wing/room/original_text/valid_until through the merged result dict.

commit.message:
fix: security audit — path traversal, state machine, input validation, credential hardening (#2)

* fix: security audit — path traversal, state machine, input validation, credential hardening

## Fixes
- artifact_routes: sanitize filenames + path traversal guard on upload/download
- file_size_scan: block absolute paths and .. traversal in lint endpoint
- task_instructions: remove hardcoded dev-local auth fallback token
- plan_routes_ext: add wave status whitelist validation
- gates: replace catch-all _ => Ok(()) with explicit status whitelist
- plan_routes/task_routes: add max length validation on string inputs

## ADRs
- Copy ADR-045 (plan state machine) from monorepo
- Create ADR-002 (security audit fixes) documenting all findings
